steps.title: Identify the reinforcing relationship
steps.content: The strongest clue: both sentences advance the same argument about photography's superiority—the second elaborates it without contradicting.

steps.title: Apply the reinforcement transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is addition/reinforcement. Sentence 1 states photography's use of lines elevates it to fine art; Sentence 2 doubles down by calling linear forms photography's "indisputable and exclusive domain." The blank needs a transition word signaling that the second sentence continues and strengthens the same argument.
  • Choice A — "Indeed,": signals reinforcement or emphasis of what was just said, so it fits perfectly — Yalenti's second claim amplifies his first. ✓
  • Choice B — "In any case,": signals dismissal of a prior consideration and a pivot to something that holds regardless — no such pivot exists here. ✗
  • Choice C — "However,": signals contrast — but the second sentence does not contradict the first. ✗
  • Choice D — "Even so,": signals concession despite an obstacle — there is no obstacle in Sentence 1 for Sentence 2 to overcome. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
